Figure 7. Visualization results for novel view synthesis on Ricoh360.

Figure 2. An overview of our PFGS360 framework. (a) The PFGS360 model first predicts the monocular depth map of the initial panoramic frame. (b) The omnidirectional 3DGS is then initialized using reprojected 3D points derived from the estimated depth. (c) Subsequently, both the spherical consistency–aware pose estimation (SCA-PE, Section 3.1) and depth-inlier–aware densification (DIA-Densify, Section 3.2) are performed in a frame-by-frame manner to sequentially fuse panoramic frames and optimize the Gaussian [24] representations.
Table 1. Quantitative comparison results for novel view synthesis on OB3D.
Table 2. Quantitative comparison results for camera pose estimation on OB3D.
